methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $2.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $250M - $32.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Liquid AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series A), post-money $2.35B

private.name: Twelve Labs
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series A), post-money $250M

data.narrative:
Field AI, founded by Ali Agha and Shayegan Omidshafiei, is a Series A stage AI & Software company with a current post-money valuation of $2.0B and $405M total raised to date. The valuation assessment draws on a peer universe of 9 same-sector and adjacent-stage companies, comprising 1 public comparable (Palantir) and 8 private peers including Liquid AI, Physical Intelligence, World Labs, and Safe Superintelligence, with valuations ranging from $250M to $32.0B.

Two valuation methods were applied. The stage-progression approach applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier to the $2.0B Series A post-money, implying a $4.0B forward valuation. The peer-median method identifies a $2.4B median across the 9-company comp set, anchoring to transaction activity in the frontier AI subsector. These methods triangulate to a range with a low bound of $2.0B (current valuation floor), a median of $3.2B, and a high bound of $4.6B.

The $3.2B median suggests modest upside from current post-money, supported by peer comparables rather than aggressive stage-based multipliers. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion within the peer set ($250M to $32.0B), the early-stage nature of revenue validation in frontier AI, and the limited number of direct-stage comparables at Series A valuation. The analysis supports a fair-value range of $2.0B to $4.6B, with $3.2B as the central estimate.
